---
title: "Publications"
pagetitle: "Publications — Félix P. Leiva"
toc: false
page-layout: full
---

::: {.pubs-page}

::: {.pubs-side}
## Selected publications

[Preprints and earlier work are listed on my [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=Cgm7_bMAAAAJ) profile.]{.pubs-link}
:::

::: {.pubs-list}

::: {.pub}
{{< fa file-lines >}} [**Leiva FP**, Molina-Venegas R, Alter K, Freire CA, Hendriks AJ, Hermaniuk A, Serre-Fredj L,
Shokri M, Czarnoleski M & Mark FC]{.pub-authors} (2026). [ErythroCite: a database on red blood cell size of
fishes](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41597-026-06904-1). *Scientific Data*, **13**.

::: {.pub-buttons}
[Code](https://felixpleiva.github.io/ErythroCite/){.pub-btn}
[Data](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18543381){.pub-btn}
:::
:::

::: {.pub}
{{< fa file-lines >}} [Verberk WCEP, Henry E, **Leiva FP**, Barbarossa V & Schipper A]{.pub-authors} (2026). [Heat tolerance and its plasticity in freshwater and marine fishes reflect exposure to extremes and seasonal variation
in habitat temperatures. Heat tolerance and its plasticity in freshwater and marine fishes
reflect exposure to extremes and seasonal variation in habitat temperatures]( https://doi.org/10.1111/ele.70341). *Ecology Letters*, **29**,e70341.

::: {.pub-buttons}
[Code](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18266037){.pub-btn}
[Data](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18266037){.pub-btn}
:::
:::


::: {.pub}
{{< fa file-lines >}} [Kempf S, Neven CJ, Timpe A, **Leiva FP**, Seibel B & Mark FC]{.pub-authors} (2025). [The physiological response of the Arctic key species Polar cod, *Boreogadus saida*, to hypoxia in a warming ocean: critical
oxygen levels and swimming performance](https://doi.org/10.1186/s40850-025-00241-3). *BMC Zoology*, **10**,1–12.

::: {.pub-buttons}
[Code](#){.pub-btn}
[Data](https://doi.org/10.1594/PANGAEA.962684){.pub-btn}
:::
:::

::: {.pub}
{{< fa file-lines >}} [**Leiva FP**, Ellers J, Berg MP, Cuxart-Erruz R, Barneche D, Blackburn T, Castañeda LE, Chown
S, Gaitán-Espitia JD, Gebauer P, Gomez Isaza D, Hardy I, Hermaniuk A, Hirst A, Jorissen S,
Keasar T, Koene JM, Le Lann C, Martorelli I, Molinet C, Niklitschek EJ, Oliveira B, Olivier B,
Orizaola G, Pilakouta N, Shameer KS, Shokri M, Stoks R, Tougeron K, Tuni C, van de Pol ILE, van
Dis NE, Visser B, Vogels J, White CR, Wu N & Verberk WCEP]{.pub-authors} (2025). [ShareTrait: towards
interoperable and reusable individual trait-based data in ectotherms](https://doi.org/10.1111/1365-2435.70147). *Functional Ecology*, **39**, 3124–3138.

::: {.pub-buttons}
[Code](https://github.com/ShareTraitProject/ShareTrait){.pub-btn}
[Data](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.14826294){.pub-btn}
:::
:::

::: {.pub}
{{< fa file-lines >}} [**Leiva FP**, Santos M, Rezende EL & Verberk WCEP]{.pub-authors} (2024). [Intraspecific variation of heat tolerance in a model ectotherm: the role of oxygen, cell size and body size](https://doi.org/10.1111/1365-2435.14485). *Functional Ecology*, **38**, 439–448.

::: {.pub-buttons}
[Code](https://github.com/felixpleiva/TDT_DGRP_lines){.pub-btn}
[Data](https://github.com/felixpleiva/TDT_DGRP_lines){.pub-btn}
:::
:::

::: {.pub}
{{< fa file-lines >}} [**Leiva FP**, Boerrigter JGJ & Verberk WCEP]{.pub-authors} (2023). [The role of cell size in shaping responses to oxygen and temperature in fruit flies](https://doi.org/10.1111/1365-2435.14294). *Functional Ecology*.

::: {.pub-buttons}
[Code](https://github.com/felixpleiva/Plastic_responses_DGRP_lines){.pub-btn}
[Data](https://github.com/felixpleiva/Plastic_responses_DGRP_lines){.pub-btn}
:::
:::

::: {.pub}
{{< fa file-lines >}} [Babin CH, **Leiva FP**, Verberk WCEP & Rees BB]{.pub-authors} (2023). Evolution of key oxygen-sensing genes is associated with hypoxia tolerance in fishes. *Journal of Experimental Biology*.

::: {.pub-buttons}
[Code](https://github.com/felixpleiva/Genetic_basis_Pcrit){.pub-btn}
[Data](https://github.com/felixpleiva/Genetic_basis_Pcrit){.pub-btn}
:::
:::

::: {.pub}
{{< fa file-lines >}} [Verberk WCEP, Sandker JF, van de Pol ILE, Urbina MA, Wilson RW, McKenzie DJ & **Leiva FP**]{.pub-authors} (2022). [Body mass and cell size shape the tolerance of fishes to low oxygen in a temperature-dependent manner](https://doi.org/10.1111/gcb.16319). *Global Change Biology*, **28**, 5695–5707.

::: {.pub-buttons}
[Code](https://github.com/felixpleiva/Hypoxia_tolerance_fishes){.pub-btn}
[Data](https://github.com/felixpleiva/Hypoxia_tolerance_fishes){.pub-btn}
:::
:::

::: {.pub}
{{< fa file-lines >}} [Verspagen N, **Leiva FP**, Janssen I & Verberk WCEP]{.pub-authors} (2020). Effects of developmental plasticity on heat tolerance may be mediated by changes in cell size in *Drosophila melanogaster*. *Insect Science*.

::: {.pub-buttons}
[Code](#){.pub-btn}
[Data](#){.pub-btn}
:::
:::

::: {.pub}
{{< fa file-lines >}} [Toledo P, Niklitschek EJ, Darnaude AM, **Leiva FP** et al.]{.pub-authors} (2020). The trophic ecology of partial migration: insights from *Merluccius australis* off NW Patagonia. *ICES Journal of Marine Science*, **77**, 1927–1940.

::: {.pub-buttons}
[Code](#){.pub-btn}
[Data](#){.pub-btn}
:::
:::

::: {.pub}
{{< fa file-lines >}} [Sunday J, Bennett JM, Calosi P, Clusella-Trullas S, Gravel S, **Leiva FP** et al.]{.pub-authors} (2019). [Thermal tolerance patterns across latitude and elevation](https://doi.org/10.1098/rstb.2019.0036). *Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society B: Biological Sciences*, **374**, 20190036.

::: {.pub-buttons}
[Code](#){.pub-btn}
[Data](#){.pub-btn}
:::
:::

::: {.pub}
{{< fa file-lines >}} [**Leiva FP**, Calosi P & Verberk WCEP]{.pub-authors} (2019). [Scaling of thermal tolerance with body mass and genome size in ectotherms: a comparison between water- and air-breathers](https://doi.org/10.1098/rstb.2019.0035). *Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society B: Biological Sciences*, **374**, 20190035.

::: {.pub-buttons}
[Code](#){.pub-btn}
[Data](#){.pub-btn}
:::
:::

::: {.pub}
{{< fa file-lines >}} [Toledo P, Darnaude AM, Niklitschek EJ, Ojeda V, Voué R, **Leiva FP** et al.]{.pub-authors} (2019). Partial migration and early size of southern hake *Merluccius australis*. *ICES Journal of Marine Science*, **76**, 1094–1106.

::: {.pub-buttons}
[Code](#){.pub-btn}
[Data](#){.pub-btn}
:::
:::

::: {.pub}
{{< fa file-lines >}} [**Leiva FP**, Garcés C, Verberk WCEP, Care M, Paschke K & Gebauer P]{.pub-authors} (2018). Differences in the respiratory response to temperature and hypoxia across four life-stages of the intertidal porcelain crab *Petrolisthes laevigatus*. *Marine Biology*, **165**, 146.

::: {.pub-buttons}
[Code](https://github.com/felixpleiva/respiration_life_stages_crabs){.pub-btn}
[Data](https://github.com/felixpleiva/respiration_life_stages_crabs){.pub-btn}
:::
:::

::: {.pub}
{{< fa file-lines >}} [**Leiva FP**, Niklitschek EJ, Paschke K, Gebauer P & Urbina MA]{.pub-authors} (2016). Tide-related biological rhythm in the oxygen consumption rate of ghost shrimp *Neotrypaea uncinata*. *Journal of Experimental Biology*, **219**, 1957–1960.

::: {.pub-buttons}
[Code](https://github.com/felixpleiva/Respiration_ghost_shrimp_tidal_cycles){.pub-btn}
[Data](https://github.com/felixpleiva/Respiration_ghost_shrimp_tidal_cycles){.pub-btn}
:::
:::

::: {.pub}
{{< fa file-lines >}} [**Leiva FP**, Urbina MA, Cumillaf JP, Gebauer P & Paschke K]{.pub-authors} (2015). Physiological responses of the ghost shrimp *Neotrypaea uncinata* to oxygen availability and recovery after severe environmental hypoxia. *Comparative Biochemistry and Physiology Part A: Molecular & Integrative Physiology*.

::: {.pub-buttons}
[Code](https://github.com/felixpleiva/Ghost_shrimp_respiration_reoxygentation){.pub-btn}
[Data](https://github.com/felixpleiva/Ghost_shrimp_respiration_reoxygentation){.pub-btn}
:::
:::

:::

:::
